  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "CSST (Corrugated Stainless Steel Tubing) gas piping is not bonded, which creates a risk of arcing and perforation from nearby lightning strikes or electrical faults.",
    "Lack of bonding does not comply with manufacturer instructions or code requirements for CSST systems."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately stop work and isolate gas supply if arcing or electrical hazards are suspected.",
    "Install a listed bonding clamp and connect it with a minimum 6 AWG copper conductor to the building's grounding electrode system per CSST manufacturer's instructions and NFPA 54/IFGC.",
    "Verify bonding continuity and attachment point is accessible and permanent before returning system to operation."
  ],
  "rationale": "Unbonded CSST is a known fire and explosion hazard. IFGC Section 310 and NFPA 54 require gas piping systems to be bonded in accordance with the electrical code and manufacturer's installation instructions, because CSST is susceptible to lightning-induced electrical arcing that can puncture the tubing. The condition is non-compliant and immediately dangerous.",
  "references": [
    "IFGC 310.1 (Electrical Bonding)",
    "NFPA 54 (National Fuel Gas Code) Section 7.13",
    "CSST manufacturer installation instructions (e.g., Gastite, TracPipe)"
  ]
}
